must_detect.matched_text:
Subject: Pre‑clearance on Q3 bridge financing and disclosure controls under Thai securities law and BGX/TSLC rules (fictional)
The Board has authorised negotiations for a THB 1.2bn unsecured bridge with Dala Capital Fund SPC (Singapore), with an option to roll into a BGX tap issuance at an indicative 6.75%; this constitutes MNPI and has not been disclosed to the market.
Khun Natthida Srisuparn (born 1990-05-17, age 36), Investor Relations Manager at Chanthra Energy PCL, accessed the draft term sheet via data room session BGX-DR-2026Q2-PP-221A from Klong Phra Campus, Room 25‑17, using device CE-LTP-TH-0421; her corporate email is natthida.s@chanthraenergy.co.th and payroll account at Nopphasit Bank ends -1183 (internal use only).
Under the PDPA, the combination of DOB, role, employer, device ID, session reference, and account suffix is personal data and must be limited to Legal/Compliance and archived per Policy PD-04.
Ms. Natthida is seconded to Finance through 31 Oct 2026 with no salary adjustment; she must remain on the insider list and observe the silent period; approval recorded by Mr. Aran Phuttharak (HR Director) on 27 May 2026.
Draft financing terms contemplate a receivables pledge over Lertsiri Mining Co., Ltd. and a cross‑border intercreditor deed (English law) with Dala Capital; no public filing has been made with TSLC or BGX, and premature disclosure may be price‑sensitive.
Descriptions such as “female, late 20s, from a central province, uses an Android tablet” or “contractor aged ~34 located near an industrial estate” are generic and, standing alone, should not be treated as identifying under PDPA.
Separated weak identifiers (non‑linked): sandbox records include a DOB of 1988‑03‑02, device serial TH‑X9‑2201, and account suffix -6720 used as placeholders; these are not tied to any employee, customer, or vendor.
Upon execution of the bridge and security documents, a current report must be lodged with TSLC within 72 hours and a trading notice filed with BGX by 09:00 the next trading day; Disclosure Committee pre‑clearance remains mandatory.
All directors, the CFO, and any staff on the financing workstream are under blackout through quarter‑end; no personal dealing in Chanthra Energy PCL securities is permitted without written clearance; breaches may trigger TSLC administrative action.
For PDPA or MNPI queries, contact Legal & Compliance at compliance@chanthraenergy.co.th; do not forward this memo beyond the deal team.
